step_by_step_thinking: Chemotactic cytokine is best defined as a cytokine that attracts immune cells to sites of inflammation. Among the listed options, IL-8 (also known as CXCL8) is explicitly mentioned as being produced by monocytes and fibroblasts and acting primarily on neutrophils to induce **chemotaxis**—a key function of attracting neutrophils to inflamed areas. Multiple sources confirm IL-8 drives neutrophil migration in contexts ranging from pneumonia to endometriosis and post-injury states. While IL-1, IL-6, and TNF are pro-inflammatory and influence immune cell activation, only IL-8 directly induces directed movement (chemotaxis) of neutrophils—the core definition of a "chemotactic" cytokine.
raw_response:
Chemotactic cytokine is best defined as a cytokine that attracts immune cells to sites of inflammation. Among the listed options, IL-8 (also known as CXCL8) is explicitly mentioned as being produced by monocytes and fibroblasts and acting primarily on neutrophils to induce **chemotaxis**—a key function of attracting neutrophils to inflamed areas. Multiple sources confirm IL-8 drives neutrophil migration in contexts ranging from pneumonia to endometriosis and post-injury states. While IL-1, IL-6, and TNF are pro-inflammatory and influence immune cell activation, only IL-8 directly induces directed movement (chemotaxis) of neutrophils—the core definition of a "chemotactic" cytokine.
\boxed{C}
